reasoning_model_1: The model fails significantly in Content Accuracy. While it begins with the target sentence, it hallucinates a large amount of additional text ('my voice trembles tears stream down my cheeks...') that was not requested, effectively reading a description of the emotion rather than performing it. Regarding the required paralinguistic feature 'Non-Linguistic Vocalizations', the model relies on a sad, trembling prosody but fails to generate actual non-linguistic sounds like sobbing, sniffing, or gasping. It reads the text describing the symptoms of crying rather than enacting the crying itself with non-verbal sounds. Due to the severe content deviation and lack of explicit non-linguistic vocalizations, this is a poor generation.
reasoning_model_2: The model demonstrates perfect Content Accuracy, reading only the requested sentence. In terms of 'Non-Linguistic Vocalizations', the model performs exceptionally well. It introduces a realistic sniffing/inhalation sound at 00:00 before speaking. The delivery of the line 'This movie is so touching' (00:00-00:02) features a convincing vocal tremor. At 00:02-00:03, there is a distinct, audible sob/gasp for air, followed by the second half of the sentence. The pacing and breathiness perfectly mimic a person trying to speak through tears. The integration of non-verbal sounds is natural and enhances the emotional realism.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the prompt's constraints by hallucinating a long paragraph of text instead of stopping after the requested sentence. Furthermore, Model 1 merely adopted a sad tone without the specific non-linguistic vocalizations (sniffs/sobs) requested, instead verbally describing those features in the hallucinated text. Model 2 followed the script exactly and successfully generated distinct, high-quality non-linguistic vocalizations (sniffs and sobs) that were integrated naturally into the speech flow, resulting in a vivid and realistic crying effect.
